content.full_text:
FPPC

Dec 6, 1 45 PM 90

CITY OF STOCKTON
OFFICE OF
THE CITY ATTORNEY
425 North El Dorado Street
Stockton, California 95202-1997

Telephone (209) 944-8333
Telefax    (209) 944-8898

DEPUTIES
Barbara J. Anderson      Michael T. Rushwain
Cynthia L Humbert       Lauren P. Stout
Guy D. Petzold          Bart J. Thilgen
                        Mark E. Tribble

INVESTIGATOR
Steven J. Hunt

R. Thomas Harris
City Attorney

John T. Moran
Assistant City Attorney


                                    December 5, 1990


     John Rothman, Esq.
     FPPC--Legal Division
     428 J Street, Suite 800
     Sacramento CA  95814

          RE:  CONFIRMATION OF OPINION RECEIVED BY TELEPHONE

     Dear Mr. Rothman:

          On December 5, 1990, I presented to you at the request
     of Stockton City Councilmember Mel Panizza the following
     fact situation and question:

          A  councilmember  owns  a  landscaping  and  gardening
     business.  For the past twelve years he has had a
     contract for landscape maintenance with a local
     homeowners association. One of the nine members of the
     Board of Directors of the homeowners association has been
     a director for the past three years and is now a
     candidate for a high paying City job which will be filled
     by council vote.  This person, while a member of the
     Board of Directors, has never voted on the council-
     member's landscaping contract as the contract was already
     in place when he first became a director and none of its
     provisions have since been changed.  Can the council-
     member vote on this person's appointment to the City job?

          This is to confirm that it is your opinion as stated
     today that the councilmember could vote when the
     appointment of the homeowners' Board member to a city
     position is before the City Council. The basis of your
     opinion was the fact that the councilmember was receiving
     income from the homeowners association as an entity and
     not from the individual members of its board. You also
     stated that this opinion was bolstered by the fact that

John Rothman, Esq.
December 5, 1990
Page 2

the board member has never even been called upon to vote
on the councilmember's landscape contract.

Please confirm my understanding of your opinion.  Thank
you.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        R. THOMAS HARRIS
                                        CITY ATTORNEY


                                        By [signature]
                                           JOHN T. MORAN
                                           ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY

JTM:gc

embedding.qa_text: This is a letter from the City of Stockton Office of the City Attorney, dated December 5, 1990, addressed to John Rothman, Esq., FPPC--Legal Division, Sacramento CA. The letter confirms an opinion received by telephone regarding a councilmember's ability to vote on the appointment of a homeowners' Board member to a City position. The councilmember owns a landscaping and gardening business and has had a contract for landscape maintenance with a local homeowners association for twelve years. One of the nine members of the Board of Directors of the homeowners association has been a director for three years and is now a candidate for a high-paying City job which will be filled by council vote. This person, while a member of the Board of Directors, has never voted on the councilmember's landscaping contract as it was already in place when he first became a director and none of its provisions have since been changed. The letter states that the councilmember could vote when the appointment of the homeowners' Board member to a City position is before the City Council. The basis of this opinion is that the councilmember was receiving income from the homeowners association as an entity and not from individual members of its board. The opinion was bolstered by the fact that the councilmember's landscaping contract was already in place when the director became a member of the Board of Directors. John Rothman, Esq. December 5, 1990 Page 2 the board member has never even been called upon to vote on the councilmember's landscape contract. Please confirm my understanding of your opinion. Thank you. Sincerely, R. THOMAS HARRIS CITY ATTORNEY BY JOHN T. MORAN ASSISTANT CITY ATTORNEY JTM:gc
